question: An instance of `order-process.bpmn` is created with the `zbctl` command shown. According to the guide, what happens when this instance reaches the XOR gateway that branches on `orderValue`?

options.text: An incident is raised because the gateway cannot compare a string `orderValue` to an integer threshold.
The instance routes to the high-value branch because `"99"` coerces to a number above zero.
`zbctl` rejects the create command up front because `orderValue` is not an integer.
The default sequence flow out of the XOR fires silently, so no incident is reported.

a.text: The guide describes exactly this case: `orderValue` is present but set as a string, so the XOR gateway cannot compare it to the integer threshold and the engine raises an incident.

b.text: FEEL does not silently coerce strings to numbers for `>=` comparisons; the comparison fails with a type error rather than matching a branch.

c.text: `zbctl` accepts the JSON payload as written and creates the instance; the failure surfaces later when the gateway evaluates.

d.text: A failed gateway evaluation creates an incident on the gateway; the engine does not silently take a default flow when the condition fails to evaluate.

explanation: The guide demonstrates that creating an instance with `orderValue` as a string causes the XOR gateway condition `orderValue>=100` to fail with a type-comparison error, producing an incident that an operator must resolve before the instance can progress.
